title: EAFIX SSOT Registry Delivery Verification Report
purpose: Run-specific independent verdict on whether the final merged registry plan delivered every required obligation correctly and with sufficient evidence.
authority_notice: This unexecuted template is not a verification result. It cannot support a delivery or completion claim until every required gate, check, obligation, evidence reference, freshness test, and approval has been populated and validated.

gate_results.title: Input and scope integrity
gate_results.purpose: Bind the run to the exact plan and repository state before implementation claims are examined.

check_results.title: Bind verification to the exact final plan

negative_test.mutation: Change one byte in a temporary copy of the plan.
negative_test.expected_failure: Target-binding check exits nonzero and reports a hash mismatch.
negative_test.isolation_rule: Run only in a disposable fixture, temporary worktree, or test repository; restore and prove clean state afterward.

expected_results: Every required reference has an explicit resolution status.

The authority priority applied matches the target plan.

No lower-authority source is silently substituted.
The authority priority applied matches the target plan.

gate_results.on_fail: Publish verified_incomplete or verification_blocked. Never publish verified_complete.

finding_contract.rule: A residual finding may remain only when it does not contradict the claimed scope or any definition-of-done obligation and has owner, rationale, severity, follow-up, and approval when required.

controls.detection: Require integration evidence and consumer references for every output where integration is applicable.
controls.blocking_when: A required deliverable has no verified consumer, generator, validator, routing, or governance connection.

controls.detection: Regenerate and compare bytes; inspect provenance and managed-output manifest.
controls.blocking_when: Committed generated bytes differ from deterministic output.

controls.pattern: Validator exists but is not executed
controls.detection: Trace validator into CI, task commands, or verification transcripts.
controls.blocking_when: A required validator has no fresh execution evidence.

controls.detection: Inspect continue-on-error, report-only flags, required-check rules, and registry maturity.
controls.blocking_when: A canonical or completion-critical check can fail without blocking.

controls.detection: Inspect workflow run, jobs, steps, conclusions, and startup blockers.
controls.blocking_when: Skipped, neutral, cancelled, or startup-blocked evidence is presented as pass.

controls.detection: Compare evidence target_commit_sha and relevant input hashes to the verification context.
One-for-one reconcile baseline removals to resolution evidence and parity IDs.
controls.blocking_when: Any removal lacks a valid resolution record.

controls.pattern: Authority edited to make parity green
controls.detection: Inspect authority diffs during parity and burn-down PR classes.
controls.blocking_when: An authority edit is used to eliminate a registry divergence without separate governance approval.

controls.detection: Validate field-group ownership and active document authority state.
controls.blocking_when: More than one active source owns the same governed fact.

controls.pattern: Task marked complete without outputs or acceptance evidence
controls.detection: Join task completion records to output and acceptance obligations.
controls.blocking_when: Any task-complete record lacks all required outputs and acceptance evidence.

controls.pattern: Human decision inferred rather than recorded
controls.detection: Require identifiable approval record and decision registry reference.
controls.blocking_when: A blocking decision has no explicit approval.

controls.detection: Require executed rollback transcript and restored-state validation.
controls.blocking_when: An irreversible or migration action lacks successful rollback evidence.

controls.detection: Require a real downstream consumer and break-and-restore mutation proof.
controls.blocking_when: No required downstream consumer depends on registry data.

controls.pattern: Legacy authority remains active after cutover
controls.detection: Cross-check registry status, doc authority, routing, supersession pointers, and banners.
controls.blocking_when: A predecessor remains selected as current authority for a cut-over fact group.

controls.detection: Execute clean-clone build and compare committed/generated hashes.
controls.blocking_when: Hidden local state or uncommitted inputs are required.

controls.detection: Compare published wording to actual registry state, baseline, CI, migration scope, and check verdicts.
controls.blocking_when: The final status claims more than the evidence supports.

verified_complete_requirements.requirement: All verification gates pass in order.
All blocker and high checks pass.
All 1405 obligations are mapped and resolved.
All required evidence is fresh and hash-resolvable.
Anti-false-completion controls detect no unresolved blocking pattern.

verified_incomplete_conditions.condition: Verification executed but one or more required results failed.
Required migration, cutover, consumer, runbook, or supersession work is incomplete.

verification_blocked_conditions.condition: Verification cannot bind to the exact plan or commit.
Required routing/authority references are unavailable for dependent conclusions.
Required CI or runtime evidence cannot execute.
Evidence integrity or freshness cannot be established.

publication_rules: Use exactly one allowed final status.
Validate all gate, check, obligation, and evidence references.
Do not publish verified_complete with any blocker/high failure or blocked check.
Do not publish verified_complete unless every one of the 1,405 obligations is resolved.
Do not publish verified_complete unless the evidence manifest is sealed.
Record the report SHA-256 externally after final serialization; do not self-hash.

template_completion_instructions: Freeze run_context and verify immutable package bindings.
Execute gate results in dependency order.
Populate every check verdict and expected-result observation.
Resolve every obligation from supporting check verdicts and fresh evidence.
Run anti-false-completion, completion-policy, and integrity audits.
Seal the evidence manifest and record its SHA-256.
Record owner approval, rejection, or deferral.
Publish only an allowed final status.

integrity.serialization: UTF-8, LF newlines, 2-space indentation, trailing newline.
integrity.self_hash_policy: Do not embed this file's final SHA-256. Record it in immutable publication metadata after serialization.
